key_points: A ratio compares two quantities using division
Two ratios are proportional when their cross products are equal
Map scale represents the relation between map distance and real distance
Multi-term ratios compare more than two quantities
Pie charts represent data as sectors of a circle
Direct proportion means two quantities increase or decrease together
Inverse proportion means one quantity increases while the other decreases
examples: Map scale example: 1 cm represents 60 km
Pie chart example: 25% of data = 90° sector
formulas: Sector angle in pie chart = (value / total value) × 360°
Actual distance = map distance × scale factor
exam_tips: Always simplify ratios before solving problems
Use cross multiplication to check proportions
Remember total angle in a pie chart is 360°
Identify whether the situation is direct or inverse proportion
Write ratio shares clearly when dividing quantities
